---
title: "So I Think I Am Canadian?"
author: "Joanna R. Pepin"
date: "2026-03-13"
categories: [news]
draft: true
---

I moved to Toronto in 2023 after being hired by the University of Toronto as a family sociologist. One of the many perks of the job is the university pays for immigration lawyers to help new faculty obtain a temporary work permit and apply for permanent residency. Historically, this has meant that faculty begin the job with a temporary work permit. After 1 year, we then submit an application for permanent residency. I did that. However, Canada has altered the point system for what it takes to be eligible for permanent residency (PR) in such as a way that being over the age of 30 makes it now nearly impossible to receive an invitation to apply for PR.
